prs.title: fix(core): serialize custom properties in AxiosError.toJSON (#6511)

labels.name: status::changes-requested
labels.description: A reviewer requested changes to the PR

prs.body:
### Summary
Fixes [#6511](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/6511)

When using `AxiosError.from(...)`, it's possible to pass `customProps` which are merged onto the error instance. However, if the error was serialized via `AxiosError.toJSON()`, those custom properties were completely omitted from the output. This affected users attempting to log [AxiosError](cci:2://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/axios/lib/core/AxiosError.js:4:0-69:1) objects using JSON-based loggers like Pino.

This PR updates `AxiosError.toJSON()` to spread any enumerable own properties of the error instance into the resulting JSON object. `request`, `response`, and `cause` are intentionally excluded to prevent circular reference errors or serializing raw Error instances.

### Important Changes
- Modified `AxiosError.prototype.toJSON` to dynamically capture custom properties.
- Added a regression test in [AxiosError.spec.js](cci:7://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/axios/test/specs/core/AxiosError.spec.js:0:0-0:0) to ensure custom properties successfully serialize. 

### Testing
- [x] Verified unit tests (`npm run test:node`) pass successfully.
- [x] Verified package typing and build tests (`npm run test:package`) pass successfully.

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Serialize custom properties in `AxiosError.toJSON()` so fields from `AxiosError.from()` are kept in JSON and show up in loggers. Spread custom props before standard fields so canonical error fields always win. Fixes #6511.

## Description

- Summary of changes
  - Updated `toJSON()` to include own enumerable properties and exclude `request`, `response`, and `cause`.
  - Spread custom props first, then standard fields (`message`, `name`, etc.) to prevent overrides.
- Reasoning
  - Custom props from `AxiosError.from(...)` were dropped, hiding context like request/trace IDs in logs.
- Additional context
  - Exclusions avoid circular references and raw `Error` serialization.

## Docs

No docs changes.

## Testing

- Added unit test in `test/specs/core/AxiosError.spec.js` to assert custom props appear in `toJSON()` output.
- Existing tests pass.

<sup>Written for commit fceca01cbb3b1d797830546f6560a3af9eb7e79a. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

prs.title: fix: improve zod error formatting and yarn berry support
prs.body:
This PR improves error output in the CLI and adds better support for Yarn Berry.

Changes

Zod error formatting

Improved how validation errors are displayed in the console

Multiple messages for the same field are now separated with a period and space instead of commas

Each message is normalized to end with a period

This makes the output easier to read, especially when multiple validations fail for the same field.

Yarn Berry support

Updated getPackageRunner to correctly detect Yarn Berry environments

Uses @antfu/ni for detection and switches to yarn dlx when applicable

Existing behavior for pnpm dlx, bunx, and npx remains unchanged

Tests

Added tests for handleError to cover the updated formatting

Ran existing tests for package manager detection

Verified output manually with sample Zod errors

prs.title: fix: svelte:element hydration in raw text elements
prs.body:
Fix hydration error for raw text elements in <svelte:element>

Hydration was failing when using <svelte:element> with raw text elements like <style> and <script>.

This happened because $.append calls hydrate_next(), which expects a sibling node to move to. However, SSR doesn’t insert hydration markers inside raw text elements, so hydrate_next() ended up hitting a null sibling and throwing.

This change adds a temporary comment node during hydration so hydrate_next() has a valid sibling to work with. The comment is removed immediately after, so it doesn’t affect the final DOM.

Also added a regression test in runtime-runes to cover this case.

prs.title: fix(cli): pass ignore option to chokidar watcher to prevent watching ignored files
prs.body:
FFixes #9565 by passing the parsed babel ignore options to chokidar's ignored configuration, preventing system limit errors for wide ignore scopes like `node_modules`.
Currently, when `@babel/cli` is run with the `--watch` flag, `chokidar` is initialized without knowing which files Babel is configured to ignore. This causes it to unnecessarily track large directories (such as `node_modules`), potentially leading to `ENOSPC` file watcher limit errors. This PR ensures that `babelOptions.ignore` is properly propagated down to `chokidar`'s `ignored` array.

prs.title: fix(types): use FastifyBaseLogger for childLoggerFactory to fix strict mode assignability
prs.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have written a descriptive issue title
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure the bug has not already been reported

### Description

This PR fixes a TypeScript issue that occurs when using a `pino.Logger` instance with `fastify()` in strict mode, as reported in #4960. 

**The Problem:**
When TypeScript strict mode is enabled, assigning a [FastifyInstance](cci:2://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/fastify/types/instance.d.ts:119:0-608:1) created with a `pino.Logger` to a variable typed with the default [FastifyBaseLogger](cci:2://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/fastify/types/logger.d.ts:26:0-28:1) results in a type error. This happens because the `childLoggerFactory` property uses the generic [Logger](cci:2://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/fastify/test/types/logger.test-d.ts:39:0-41:1) as its `this` context type, making it contravariant and thus preventing the assignment.

**The Fix:**
I changed the `childLoggerFactory` property type in [types/instance.d.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/fastify/types/instance.d.ts:0:0-0:0) to use [FastifyBaseLogger](cci:2://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/fastify/types/logger.d.ts:26:0-28:1) (the constraint/base type) instead of the generic [Logger](cci:2://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/fastify/test/types/logger.test-d.ts:39:0-41:1). Since every generic [Logger](cci:2://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/fastify/test/types/logger.test-d.ts:39:0-41:1) extends [FastifyBaseLogger](cci:2://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/fastify/types/logger.d.ts:26:0-28:1), the factory remains correctly typed, but the property is no longer contravariant.

I also added a regression test in [test/types/logger.test-d.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/fastify/test/types/logger.test-d.ts:0:0-0:0) to ensure this exact assignment works correctly going forward.

Fixes #4960

prs.title: fix: correct private field type in react-devtools-fusebox package.json
prs.body:
## Summary

Fixes #35793

The `private` field in [packages/react-devtools-fusebox/package.json](cci:7://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/react/packages/react-devtools-fusebox/package.json:0:0-0:0) was 
incorrectly set to a string `"true"` instead of the boolean `true`.

The [npm specification](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v10/configuring-npm/package-json#private) 
explicitly states this field must be a boolean. Using a string is malformed 
and non-compliant, even if package managers happen to tolerate it.

## Changes

- [packages/react-devtools-fusebox/package.json](cci:7://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/react/packages/react-devtools-fusebox/package.json:0:0-0:0): `"private": "true"` → `"private": true`

## Test Plan

No functional behavior changes. This is a correctness fix to conform to the 
npm package.json specification.

prs.title: fix: route _log/_debug to stderr, simplify LINE regex, add troubleshooting docs
prs.body:
## Summary

This PR addresses three independent improvements to the dotenv codebase.

---

### Fix 1: Route [_log](cci:1://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/dotenv/lib/main.js:137:0-139:1) and [_debug](cci:1://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/dotenv/lib/main.js:133:0-135:1) output to `stderr`

**Problem:** [_log()](cci:1://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/dotenv/lib/main.js:137:0-139:1) and [_debug()](cci:1://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/dotenv/lib/main.js:133:0-135:1) both used `console.log`, which writes to `stdout`. This causes diagnostic/status messages to pollute the standard output stream, breaking common shell patterns like:

```sh
node app.js | jq         # dotenv tip messages get mixed into the JSON stream
node app.js > output.txt # log messages end up written to the file

prs.title: chore(docs): rename `docs-next` to `docs`

milestone.title: Chores
milestone.description: These issues don't change the behavior of Mocha, but might improve performance, documentation, and general developer experience for contributors to Mocha.

prs.body:
## PR Checklist

- [x] Addresses an existing open issue: fixes #5676
- [x] That issue was marked as [`status: accepting prs`](https://github.com/mochajs/mocha/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22status%3A+accepting+prs%22)
- [x] Steps in [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/mochajs/mocha/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) were taken

## Overview

This PR addresses issue #5676 by renaming the `docs-next` directory to `docs`. Since the Astro Starlight site has become the primary documentation for Mocha, the "next" suffix is no longer necessary. The legacy `docs` folder has been replaced.

### Changes Made:
* Moved `docs/_data/supporters.js` to the new docs directory as it's still required for generating the sponsors JSON.
* Replaced the obsolete `docs/` repository with `docs-next/`.
* Updated all references pointing to `docs-next` across the codebase:
  * Modified `package.json` scripts at the root and inside the docs directory.
  * Updated `.github/workflows/mocha.yml` path triggers.
  * Updated `eslint.config.js` to correct the linting target.
  * Updated `README.md` and `netlify.toml` file paths.
* Added `debug` as a `devDependency` in `docs/package.json` since `supporters.js` relies on it, fixing local ESLint errors.

### Related Issues
Fixes #5676

This PR fixes two typos in the webapp codebase:
1. `recievedStatus` -> `receivedStatus` in `webapp/channels/src/packages/mattermost-redux/src/actions/users.ts`
2. `seperately` -> `separately` in `webapp/channels/src/components/suggestion/switch_channel_provider.tsx`

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Change Impact: 🟢 Low

**Reasoning:** These are isolated typo corrections with zero behavioral changes. The `recievedStatus` → `receivedStatus` rename is a local variable fix within the `setStatus` function, and the `separately` typo is a comment correction. Both changes have no logic modifications and are covered by existing tests.

**Regression Risk:** Minimal. The changes are purely corrective (variable name and comment); no control flow, business logic, or API contracts are modified. The `setStatus` function has test coverage that validates proper status update functionality, which will continue to pass with the corrected variable name.

**QA Recommendation:** Manual QA can be skipped. These are straightforward typo fixes with zero functional impact. Automated tests (particularly the `setStatus` test in `users.test.ts`) are sufficient to validate correctness. No behavioral regression risk exists.

```release-note
NONE
```

prs.title: fix(gatsby-transformer-javascript-frontmatter): support non-exported frontmatter to enable HMR

labels.name: status: triage needed
labels.description: Issue or pull request that need to be triaged and assigned to a reviewer

prs.body:
## Summary
Fixes #35756

Currently, `gatsby-transformer-javascript-frontmatter` requires `frontmatter` to be an exported variable. However, exporting non-component objects from a page file breaks React Fast Refresh (HMR), causing full page reloads on every change.

This PR updates the plugin to support extracting `frontmatter` from non-exported variables (e.g., `const frontmatter = { ... }`). This allows developers to define frontmatter without violating Fast Refresh's requirement that files only export React components.

## Changes
- Updated [src/gatsby-node.js](cci:7://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/gatsby/packages/gatsby-transformer-javascript-frontmatter/src/gatsby-node.js:0:0-0:0) to visit [VariableDeclaration](cci:1://file:///Users/sudipkumarprasad/Desktop/gatsby/packages/gatsby-transformer-javascript-frontmatter/src/gatsby-node.js:83:6-102:7) nodes and extract `frontmatter` even if it is not exported.
- Added a regression test in `src/__tests__/gatsby-node.js` to verify that non-exported frontmatter is correctly parsed.

prs.title: Fix: use codicon id for Command Prompt default profile icon
prs.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes a small but important inconsistency in the terminal default profiles schema for Windows.

### Problem
The default profile for "Command Prompt" used `icon: Codicon.terminalCmd` (the codicon object), while schema and other defaults use the codicon ID (a string), e.g. `Codicon.terminalPowershell.id`. Using the object can cause incorrect serialization/display of the icon in settings UI.

### Fix
Change the `icon` value to use the codicon id string:
- `icon: Codicon.terminalCmd` -> `icon: Codicon.terminalCmd.id`

### Why this is safe
- Single-line, documentation/schema-consistency fix.
- No behavior or runtime logic changed.
- Low-risk and straightforward to review.

Thanks!

prs.title: docs: add example for request cancellation using AbortControllerdocs: add AbortController cancellation example
prs.body:
### Summary
Adds a minimal, clear example showing how to cancel an Axios request using the native AbortController API.

### Motivation
Axios supports request cancellation, but the current documentation does not provide a concise example using the modern AbortController. This improves clarity for developers who want to cancel requests safely, especially in frontend frameworks like React.

### Changes
- Added a new section `### Canceling a Request using AbortController` above the `## Note` section in README.md.
- Included a working JavaScript snippet demonstrating request cancellation and error handling.

### Checklist
- [x] Documentation only
- [x] No code changes that affect Axios behavior
- [x] Tested example snippet locally (non-breaking)

prs.title: fix(utils): preserve original case in setCharset (#6613)
prs.body:
<!--
Fix: Preserve original case in setCharset utility function

This PR fixes Issue #6613, where utils.setCharset() unintentionally converts the entire media type to lowercase due to how content-type.parse() normalizes values.
-->

<!--
Problem

Calling:

utils.setCharset("TEXT/PLAIN", "UTF-8")


returns:

text/plain; charset=UTF-8


This is incorrect because the original case (TEXT/PLAIN) should be preserved.

What This PR Changes

Rewrote setCharset() implementation to:

Preserve the exact case of the original media type (TEXT/PLAIN, Text/Html, etc.)

Merge existing type parameters safely

Override or add the charset parameter without altering case

Added tests for:

Uppercase media types

Mixed-case media types

Types with existing parameters

Why This Fix Is Safe

No breaking API changes.

Fully backward compatible.

Does not rely on content-type.parse() for formatting.

Only improves behavior where casing is expected to be preserved.

Fixes #6613.
-->
